pi_items.source: Verified through setup_task.sh and task_utils.sh - restore_chinook_odb() function copies from /opt/libreoffice_base_samples/chinook.odb to /home/ga/chinook.odb

Downloaded and inspected Chinook SQLite database from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database. Verified with: SELECT * FROM Invoice WHERE InvoiceId = 412; Result: Invoice 412 exists (CustomerId: 58, Date: 2025-12-22, Location: Delhi, India). Total invoices in database: 412 (highest ID is 412).

Verified through logic: Current highest InvoiceId is 412 (verified above). Starting sequence at 1000 is a reasonable choice as it exceeds all existing IDs and provides clear separation between old and new records. HSQLDB command syntax verified from export_result.sh: 'ALTER TABLE "Invoice" ALTER COLUMN "InvoiceId" RESTART WITH 1000'
Downloaded and inspected Chinook SQLite database. Query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM InvoiceLine WHERE InvoiceId = 412; Result: 1 invoice line (LineId: 2240, TrackId: 3177, UnitPrice: 1.99, Quantity: 1)
Installation script references download URLs from lerocha/chinook-database GitHub repository. Verified by downloading and inspecting actual database.

pi_items.verified_value: HSQLDB 1.8 embedded in ODF (LibreOffice Base ODB format)
pi_items.source: Verified from create_chinook_odb.py script: converts SQLite to HSQLDB 1.8 embedded format. ODB file is a ZIP archive containing HSQLDB database/script file.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the Chinook database, a well-known real sample database publicly available from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database. Invoice ID 412 is the highest-numbered invoice in the standard Chinook dataset (of 412 total invoices) and has exactly 1 associated invoice line. The target path /home/ga/chinook.odb is correctly configured in the task setup. Resetting the auto-increment sequence to 1000 is a sensible choice given the current maximum invoice ID of 412. The database file is in HSQLDB 1.8 embedded format within a LibreOffice Base ODB container.
